papers.name: Multiple Choice
papers.coverage_notes: All syllabus content; questions may include calculations.

papers.name: Structured Written Paper
papers.coverage_notes: Five questions worth 20 marks each; stimulus material followed by tasks such as calculations, completing cash books, preparing accounts, suspense accounts and journal entries.

assessment_objectives.name: Knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of facts, terms, principles, policies, procedures and techniques; apply this knowledge in various accounting situations.

assessment_objectives.name: Analysis
assessment_objectives.description: Select relevant data; order, analyse and present information in an appropriate accounting form.

assessment_objectives.name: Evaluation
assessment_objectives.description: Interpret and evaluate accounting information and draw reasoned conclusions.

difficulty_ladder.description: single concept recall or direct application (e.g., define a term, calculate a simple ratio)
multi-step application within one topic (e.g., prepare ledger accounts with discounts)
cross-topic reasoning or exam-style synthesis (e.g., prepare financial statements from incomplete records with adjustments)

lesson_use.notes: v1 skill supports classification and qbank routing only. Teacher/student/parent modes are not enabled.

exclude_from_questions: incomplete records for partnerships or companies

marking_guidance.notes: Command words are defined in the syllabus p.18-19. Use generated marking guidance only; do not copy official mark schemes.
